title: NOC 32111 – Dental Hygienists and Dental Therapists | GTR Immigration
description: Dental Hygienists and Dental Therapists under NOC 32111 are essential oral healthcare professionals across Canada. With growing awareness of preventative dental care, public health initiatives, and increasing demand for community-based oral health services, this TEER 2 occupation remains a key component of Canada’s healthcare system. Qualified and licensed professionals in this field may be eligible under Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs, making NOC 32111 a practical pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group321 – Technical occupations in health (except practitioners of natural healing)Minor Group3211 – Technical occupations in dental health careNOC Code32111

duties: Conduct oral health assessments and patient screenings
Perform teeth cleaning, scaling, and fluoride treatments
Provide oral hygiene education and nutritional counseling
Assist in restorative and orthodontic procedures
Participate in community oral health programs
Refer patients for advanced dental care
employment_requirements: Dental hygienists: Completion of a two- to three-year accredited dental hygiene program
Dental therapists: Completion of a two- to three-year approved dental therapy diploma
Mandatory licensing with provincial or territorial regulatory body
College of Dental Hygienists of Ontario
College of Dental Hygienists of British Columbia
College of Registered Dental Hygienists of Alberta
Ordre des hygiénistes dentaires du Québec
Dental therapist licensure is required in Saskatchewan, Yukon, Northwest Territories, and Nunavut.
Internationally educated applicants must complete credential recognition and licensing exams before practice.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $52,000 to $105,900, depending on province, experience, and scope of practice.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 32111 is moderate nationwide. While overall supply may exceed demand nationally, opportunities remain favorable in:
Professionals with Canadian licensing and strong clinical skills may experience better employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: Dental Hygienists and Dental Therapists are employed in:

While national supply may exceed openings, regional demand remains strong in provinces such as Alberta, British Columbia, Saskatchewan, Ontario, and northern territories where access to dental services is prioritized.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 32111?
Dental Hygienists and Dental Therapists benefit from:
Opportunities for independent practice in some provinces
Access to universal healthcare and settlement benefits
Preventative dental care continues to expand across public and private sectors in Canada.
pathways: If you are a qualified Dental Hygienist or Dental Therapist, NOC 32111 may provide a pathway toward permanent residence in Canada.
